smoke_environment: Xcode iPad (A16) simulator, manual play-through by user
divergence: build + unit tests + static screenshots were green, but actually PLAYING the app surfaced gameplay defects none of that exercised

findings.bug: metronome stops once the beat starts
findings.expected: continuous click through count-in AND groove (js/highway.js:119)
findings.cause: metronome was wired only to count-in beats via onCountInBeat; groove had none

findings.expected: tempo change rescales note timing live
findings.cause: note targetMs was baked at load from bpm; engine.bpm not @Published; readout showed lesson.bpm

findings.bug: loop feels like a hard reset, not a reel
findings.expected: continuous timeline + shadow notes for seamless rollover (js/highway.js:159)
findings.cause: loop reset note positions instead of using loopIteration offset + shadow

findings.bug: Play UI fidelity well below the web original
findings.cause: Play surface built as a functional approximation, not a faithful visual port

fix: Reworked PlaybackEngine to mirror js/highway.js animate (continuous metronome, live beat*halfBeatMs timing, seamless loop + shadow notes); added 3 regression tests; all suites green.

workflow_adherence.root_lesson: Unit tests + a static screenshot are NOT a smoke test of an interactive, time-based app. verification-gate for a rhythm game must include actually playing it (audio on, tempo changed, loop watched) — ideally an automated play-through asserting metronome ticks + note motion, not just that it compiles and renders one frame.
workflow_adherence.guard_suggestion: For time-based/interactive features, require a dynamic verification step (drive the clock + assert motion/audio events) before claiming PASS; a single screenshot proves layout, not behavior.
